comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: As a U.S. citizen and voter, I care deeply about adequate standards of care for dogs. Dogs in puppy mills are abused, neglected and live lives of misery and suffering. Breeders should not be allowed to breed dogs who live their lives in a wire cage, taken out only to breed. It is reprehensible. This country, which considers itself superior to so many other c...

Please, please, please stop the torturous conditions to which breeding female dogs are subjected. I’ve seen dogs never released from their prison cages so that they were petrified of walking on grass. Subjected to breeding after breeding, often the dog’s undersides are stretched to nearly impeding their gate. And their teeth — denied appropriate food, the do...

I provide foster care for rescued Newfoundland dogs. The rescue often receives puppy mill dogs in ill health. Most recently in my care, was an 8-y-o puppy mill female dog. My husband and I frequently transport discarded breeder dogs from the breeders to rescue. Sometimes the breeder will point to a muddy pen behind the house and tell us that's where the dog is. I support updating the Animal Welfare Act standards for dog breeding facilities based on current science. Dogs in breeding facilities should also receive regular exercise, environmental enrichment, and positive social interaction with humans and compatible dogs. I am in favor of stronger standards for the care of dogs and easing the burden on dogs used for breeding.
